events.title: Sticker Boom + Golden Blitz 3 (Simpsons)
events.blurb: Sticker Boom and the third Simpsons Golden Blitz both land July 11–12 — Boom gives +50% stickers per pack inside a 15-minute window; Golden Blitz unlocks trading of previously locked Simpsons gold stickers. Stack your wishlist NOW so you can fire the moment the Blitz window opens. These two events running back-to-back is the highest-value 24-hour window left in the Simpsons season.

events.title: The Simpsons Album
events.blurb: Simpsons album closes July 29 — just 19 days left. 21 sets, 189 stickers, 34 gold total. Third Golden Blitz confirmed for July 11–12 (tomorrow). Demand for Simpsons gold stickers is at its seasonal peak right now. If you're holding a gold dupe, list it before the Blitz opens — that's when the matching queue is longest and traders are most motivated to deal.

events.title: Krustyland Invasion (Episode Stickers Set 3)
events.blurb: Episode Set 3 runs through July 15 — only 5 days left. 9 Simpsons stickers with brutal drop rates on golds. When this ends, all unopened Episode Packs auto-convert to the next set — you lose any current-set value. Trade your dupes this week or they stop being useful for active completions.

events.title: Summer Splash
events.blurb: Summer quest chain runs all season — card dupes stack up fast during the grind. Get your H:/W: list posted before the next Cards Boom so you're first in line when supply floods the community. Gold Card Trade events are the only window to move locked golds — watch for the pop-up and have your list ready.

talkingPoints: Sticker Boom + Golden Blitz 3 land tomorrow July 11–12 in Monopoly GO. Sticker Boom gives +50% stickers per pack in a 15-minute window; Golden Blitz unlocks trading of locked Simpsons gold stickers. Have your wishlist loaded and your trade partners lined up before it opens.

The Simpsons album closes July 29 — 19 days left, and Simpsons gold sticker demand is at peak pressure. Anyone missing a gold is highly motivated. If you have a Simpsons gold dupe, you're holding the most in-demand trade asset of the week.

Krustyland Invasion Episode Set 3 ends July 15 — just 5 days. After it closes, all unopened Episode Packs auto-convert to the next set. Trade your Set 3 dupes this week or they lose their value for current completions.
Watch for the next Cards Boom in Coin Master — it drops 50% extra cards from every chest, which means a flood of duplicates. The traders who post their H:/W: *before* it hits clear the most.

Gold Card Trade events in Coin Master are the only window to move locked gold cards — typically 24-48 hours, a few times a year. If you've got golds sitting idle, that event is your one shot to convert them into a completed set.
Pro tip: scan your whole album once and SwapDeck shows you every player who's missing a card you have spare. Beats scrolling H:/W: lists by hand.
Stuck one card from finishing a set? Post it — someone here almost certainly has it as a dupe collecting dust.
Golden cards are the hardest to move solo. The Trade Chain + matching tools exist specifically to connect you with the right holder faster.

Scam reports are a constant undercurrent in Coin Master Facebook groups — there are literally groups named 'COIN MASTER TRADE - SCAMMERS BANNED'. A trade log is your best protection in any platform trade.
Board Adventure is Coin Master's standalone card-collecting app — if you're playing both, make sure your H:/W: lists cover both games so you don't miss a cross-game trade partner.

Coin Master's in-game trading requires both players to be Facebook friends — which is exactly why external matching tools exist. If you're not using a platform to find trade partners first, you're trading blind.

Scopely's MONOPOLY GO! x The Simpsons crossover is their biggest collab of 2026. The Simpsons album is drawing in lapsed players — more traders in the pool means faster matches if you're active right now.
r/MonopolyGoTrading has 110K+ subscribers actively posting trades. If you're matching off-platform, that community is your widest net for finding a specific sticker holder.
